title: Rotational Energy of a Pitcher's Forearm
body: A baseball pitcher throws the ball in a motion where there is rotation of the forearm about the elbow joint as well as other movements. The linear velocity of the ball relative to the elbow joint is $$20.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ at a distance of $$0.480$$ $$m$$ from the joint, and the moment of inertia of the forearm is $$0.500$$ $$kg m^2$$.
